task_name: Implement Local Auth Persistence Readiness Candidate Mock
goal: Implement a local deterministic Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate mock that consumes the BP-062 handoff envelope and emits a local readiness output without configuring auth, persistence, approval records, or Builder task creation.
claims: BP-063 implements a local Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate mock from the BP-062 handoff envelope.
BP-063 blocks auth configuration, persistence configuration, approval recording, task creation, execution, unsafe intents, and non-prepared Approval Readiness states.
BP-063 does not call Builder, create tasks, execute tasks, configure auth, use secrets, use DB, persist data, or record approval.
assumptions: The BP-062 contract is the source of truth for auth/persistence readiness input and output envelopes.
The first auth/persistence readiness implementation should be local and fixture-reviewed before any auth, DB, persistence, or approval action is connected.
Builder Task Create remains blocked after BP-063.
dod: examples/auth-persistence-readiness-candidate contains golden input/output fixtures for prepared, review, and blocked cases.
tools/test-auth-persistence-readiness-candidate-fixtures.cjs compares CLI output against golden fixtures.
stop_conditions: Auth implementation, secrets, persistence, approval recording, live Builder access, Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, or DB becomes necessary
Auth/Persistence Readiness mock needs to write target files instead of only returning an envelope
prior_task_findings: BP-062 defined a document-only Auth/Persistence Readiness Contract and named a local candidate mock with fixtures as the next safe step.
